# Ollama And Local Models

## Summary

Ollama and other local models are execution engines, not durable knowledge stores. Vicode should make them stronger by giving them small, source-backed context packets, clear tool contracts, and deterministic verification instead of assuming the model already knows current project facts.

## Use This When

- The task uses Ollama, a local model, or an OpenAI-compatible local endpoint.
- The task depends on current docs, APIs, package versions, release state, pricing, or news.
- A smaller model needs help from Project Knowledge, workspace memory, web research, or tools.
- The user asks why a local model missed context or hallucinated a current fact.

## Operating Rules

- Treat model weights as stale for current facts unless the app supplies retrieval, web context, or repo-local evidence.
- Put the most relevant source snippets early in context. Do not bury critical facts inside long undifferentiated dumps.
- Prefer short task packets, explicit constraints, and read-only lookup tools over very broad system prompts.
- Keep provider behavior explicit: local, hosted, and compatibility endpoints may support different context windows, tool calling, latency, and reliability.
- For small local models, ask for one concrete action at a time when possible. Long multi-objective prompts raise the odds of dropped constraints.
- Do not claim provider parity until deterministic and, where needed, live-provider validation proves it.

## Retrieval Fit

Ollama documents embeddings for semantic search, retrieval, and RAG through `/api/embed`. Use local embeddings only when deterministic markdown retrieval is not enough, and keep the canonical source in markdown or repo files.

Good defaults:

- index and query with the same embedding model
- store source path, heading, and title with every chunk
- use cosine similarity for most semantic search use cases
- combine lexical title/alias/heading matches with semantic search when possible
- keep source excerpts bounded and cite the exact file or section

## Context Discipline

Use this source order for local models:

1. current user request
2. repo-local instructions, code, tests, and docs
3. user-connected Project Knowledge
4. workspace memory and recent thread context
5. packaged Vicode guidance
6. official external sources
7. clearly labeled inference

## Sources

- Ollama embeddings: https://docs.ollama.com/capabilities/embeddings
- Ollama API reference: https://docs.ollama.com/api
